channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Proposed bill looks to make President Trump’s birthday a federal holiday
item.description: Rep. Claudia Tenney (R-N.Y.) announced Friday a proposed bill, the Establishment Act, which seeks to make President Trump’s birthday and Flag Day a federal holiday in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, ABC 4

item.title: Fearful immigrants ask Florida activist to sign guardianship papers for their children
item.description: Since President Donald Trump took office, immigrants in the U.S. illegally have changed their travel patterns and try to stay home, out of fear they could be deported.
item.dc:creator: Gisela Salomon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hamas is set to free 3 more Israeli hostages after dispute threatened to reignite war in Gaza
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Three more Israelis held captive in the Gaza Strip are on the verge of being freed Saturday as part of a shaky ceasefire deal that requires Israel to release hundreds of Palestinian prisoners in exchange.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Anger, chaos and confusion take hold as federal workers face mass layoffs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Workers across the country responded with anger and confusion Friday as they grappled with the Trump administration 's aggressive effort to shrink the size of the federal workforce by ordering agencies to lay off probationary employees who have yet to qualify for civil service protec
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, BRIAN WITTE, MIKE HOUSEHOLDER and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Black Hawk pilots may have missed air traffic directive, flown on 'bad' altitude data before crash
item.description: It is likely the Black Hawk pilots “stepped on” the communication for less than a second by pressing the microphone key to communicate back to the control tower, she said.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KTLA 5

item.title: Dozens of couples get hitched at snowy Colorado ski resort on Valentine's Day
item.description: LOVELAND SKI AREA, Colo. (AP) — There is nothing like dozens of couples saying “I do” simultaneously to warm the crisp, frigid air that grips the Colorado Rockies in the heart of winter.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS PEIPERT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's reciprocal tariffs will overturn decades of trade policy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is taking a blowtorch to the rules that have governed world trade for decades. The “reciprocal’’ tariffs that he announced Thursday are likely to create chaos for global businesses and conflict with America’s allies and adversaries alike.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN and CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Rhode Island pantry that ran out of food gets $15K donation
item.description: A Providence pantry that ran out of food took to social media asking the community for help, and it worked.
item.dc:creator: Leah Crowley, NewsNation

item.title: Fiery multi-car pileup inside quarter-mile mountain tunnel leaves at least 2 dead, several injured
item.description: A deadly crash inside the Green River tunnel in southwestern Wyoming on Friday morning forced Interstate 80 to close in both directions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why you shouldn't leave your windshield wipers up during every snowstorm
item.description: Propping windshield wipers up before a snowstorm is a common routine for many car owners, but according to AAA, it's not always the best practice.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Homeowner pleads guilty to shooting Black teen Ralph Yarl who rang the wrong doorbell
item.description: LIBERTY, Mo. (AP) — An 86-year-old Kansas City man pleaded guilty Friday to a lesser charge in the 2023 shooting of Ralph Yarl, a Black honor student who rang the white man’s doorbell by mistake, in a case that shocked the country and renewed national debate about gun policies and race in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Justice Department moves to dismiss Eric Adams case
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has formally asked a federal district court to drop its corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams (D) after federal prosecutors in Manhattan resigned rather than dismiss the case.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump calls for withholding federal money from schools and colleges that require COVID vaccines
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Schools, colleges and states that require students to be immunized against COVID-19 may be at risk of losing federal money under a White House order signed Friday by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tigers, zebras and other stuffed animals will get new homes after health concerns kept them hidden
item.description: Crocodiles, monkeys, tigers, zebras and dozens of other taxidermy animals will move to new homes after concerns about arsenic exposure forced the closure of the South Dakota museum where they had been displayed for decades.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Pentagon moves to take back troops discharged over COVID-19 vaccine refusals
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Defense Department has told the military services to reach out once again to service members who were forced out or voluntarily left the military because they refused to get the COVID vaccine and see if they want to reenlist.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Idle buses, empty Border Patrol boats: Arrests for illegal crossings fall in the Rio Grande Valley
item.description: MISSION, Texas (AP) — An idled Border Patrol bus sat empty this week, on standby for any migrants surrendering near the southern tip of Texas. Agents in two speedboats zipped past pockets of sandy shores, known landing spots for people entering the U.S.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks with Russia, as the Trump administration presses both countries to find a quick endgame to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: American beauty queen charged in ex-NFL husband's murder plot granted release from Bahamas prison: report
item.description: Georgia mom Lindsay Shiver, who is accused of plotting to hire a hitman to kill her estranged husband, ex-NFL player Robert Shiver is reportedly being released on bail.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Virginia murder-suicide victims' bodies shown on Facebook Live: Docs
item.description: Search warrants provided more insight into the deaths of a mother and her three children in Chesapeake in an apparent murder-suicide.
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Finch, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico threatens to sue Google over Gulf of Mexico name change
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum on Thursday threatened to sue Google over its decision to update its maps platform to reflect President Donald Trump’s decision to rename the Gulf of Mexico as the “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WGN 9

item.title: Drone damages outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant; Russia denies responsibility
item.description: A drone armed with a warhead hit the outer protective shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, damaging the structure and briefly starting a fire.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration begins sweeping layoffs with probationary workers, warns of larger cuts to come
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration on Thursday intensified its sweeping efforts to shrink the size of the federal workforce, the nation's largest employer, by ordering agencies to lay off nearly all probationary employees who had not yet gained civil service protection — potentially affectin
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas executes man for 2004 double killing in a dispute with his strip club manager
item.description: HUNTSVILLE, Texas (AP) — A Texas man who killed his strip club manager and another man, then later prompted a massive lockdown of the state prison system when he used a cellphone smuggled onto death row to threaten a lawmaker, was executed Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and MICHAEL GRACZYK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Over 100 vehicles involved in Oregon highway crash amid 'whiteout' conditions
item.description: More than 100 cars, trucks and semi-trucks were involved in a crash along Oregon's Interstate 84 early Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Plante, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida man accused of luring young girls to home using pet ferrets
item.description: A Clearwater, Florida, man was charged with inappropriately touching young girls after allegedly using pet ferrets to lure them to his home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DeSantis signs sweeping immigration laws for Florida as states rush to fulfill Trump's agenda
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis signed a sweeping package of immigration laws on Thursday aimed at carrying out President Donald Trump ’s mass deportation agenda.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, STEPHANY MATAT, KIM CHANDLER and CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As DOGE hammers away at the US government, Republicans stir with quiet objections
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Sen. Katie Britt has been working to make sure the Trump administration's Department of Government Efficiency doesn't hit what she called “life-saving, groundbreaking research at high-achieving institutions,” including her state's beloved University of Alabama.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California city passes controversial homeless camping ban
item.description: The ordinance, which allows the city to fine those who help the homeless and makes it illegal for homeless people to camp on public property, was approved in a 6-1 vote after more than two hours of public comments.
item.dc:creator: Joey Horta, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: School gym roof collapses under 350 tons of snow
item.description: A recent snowstorm that pummeled parts of Oregon caused the roof at a high school gym to collapse under the weight of about 700,000 pounds of snow, school officials said.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, Fox 5

item.title: Jack the Ripper's identity revealed after DNA match, historian says
item.description: A historian believes he has determined Jack the Ripper's identity through a DNA match of a shawl found at the scene of one of his murders.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump doubles down on plan to empty Gaza. This is what he has said and what's at stake
item.description: Behind U.S. President Donald Trump’s vows to turn Gaza into a “Riviera of the Middle East” lies a plan to forcibly drive a population from its land, rights groups say, warning it could be a war crime under international law.
item.dc:creator: LEE KEATH Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Flu activity now highest in 15 years: See which states have it worst
item.description: Influenza cases continue to mount with the virus reaching activity levels in the U.S. not seen for the last 15 years, according to the Centers from Disease Control and Prevention.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: With public broadcasters pressured by the Trump administration, PBS shuts down its diversity office
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — PBS says it is shutting down its office of diversity, equity and inclusion to comply with President Donald Trump's executive order, firing the two executives brought on when the effort was begun in 2021.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4
